title: acaena pinnatifida subsp. longifolia
intro: Acaena pinnatifida subsp. longifolia, also known as the New Zealand thyme or prickly pear, is a type of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae) that is native to New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Acaena pinnatifida subsp. longifolia include reducing inflammation, treating wounds and skin conditions, and aiding in digestion. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
